title: District Zero EP01 Premium Critical Shots v1
objective: Validate premium storyboard quality on a tiny Nara-centered subset before any wider paid run.

shots.label: Nara corridor introduction
shots.why: Best first identity lock test for Nara under motion inside the lower transit corridor.
shots.prompt: Nara Voss runs through a leaking maintenance corridor as her wrist display flashes a pressure alert, in INT. LOWER TRANSIT STACK - SERVICE CORRIDOR - NIGHT.

shots.label: Nara valve chamber
shots.why: Tests Nara in a harder industrial set with machinery scale, vapor, and worker separation.
shots.prompt: Nara stabilizes a failing valve while exhausted workers keep their distance, in INT. PRESSURE VALVE CHAMBER - NIGHT.

shots.label: Nara and Mira in service spine
shots.why: Best cheap test of a two-character frame and the buried service-spine location depth.
shots.prompt: Nara and Mira descend into an old service corridor buried in the skeleton of the city, in INT. SEALED SERVICE SPINE - NIGHT.

shots.label: Outside world reveal
shots.why: Highest-value environment reveal for premium world scale and cinematic spectacle.
shots.prompt: Ancient shutters peel open and reveal organized lights far beyond the wall, in INT. EXTERNAL OBSERVATION CHAMBER - CONTINUOUS.

shots.label: Nara returns home
shots.why: Best low-cost emotional shot for testing acting, wet costume continuity, and pre-dawn apartment mood.
shots.prompt: Nara returns home soaked and shaken, in INT. VOSS APARTMENT - PRE-DAWN.

notes: This set stays mostly Nara-anchored because Nara is the only character already locked with a real hero reference image.
SCN_008_SHOT_001 intentionally stresses a two-character frame so Mira can be evaluated before a larger paid batch.
Storyboard CLI output should be an explicit JSON file path, not a directory path.
Use storyboard-only first; pay for video only after at least 4 of 5 frames feel premium enough to keep.
